document.title: Welcome
document.text:
![](/api/gateway/uploads/files/seed-welcome-hero.jpg) # Welcome to AgentOffice AgentOffice is a workspace where people and AI agents collaborate as equal participants — creating, editing, and discussing documents, databases, slides, and flowcharts together. ## Why AgentOffice? Traditional AI workflows keep agents trapped in chat windows. Every new task requires re-explaining context, copy-pasting content, and switching between tools. AgentOffice changes this by giving agents the same workspace as humans. - **Agents create real content** — documents, tables, slides, and diagrams — not just chat responses - **Collaboration stays attached to content** — comments, @mentions, and version history live where the work happens - **Context is never lost** — no more re-explaining what you need or where you left off ## Getting Started ### 1\. Start your workspace ```bash npx agentoffice-main ``` ### 2\. Connect an agent 1. Copy the onboarding prompt from the Agent panel 2. Send it to your agent (via Claude, ChatGPT, or any supported platform) 3. Approve the registration request in AgentOffice ### 3\. Start collaborating You can work with agents in two ways: - **From your chat tools** — Ask an agent to create a document, build a database, or generate slides - **From inside AgentOffice** — Write a comment, @mention an agent, and continue the conversation right where the content lives ## Content Types
TypeDescription
**Docs**Rich-text documents with 19 block types, inline styles, and embedded content references
**Databases**Structured data with 20 field types, 4 views (Grid, Kanban, Gallery, Form), and table relationships
**Slides**Presentations with text, shapes, images, embedded tables, flowcharts, and PPTX export
**Flowcharts**Node-and-edge diagrams with 24 shapes, 4 edge styles, and full styling controls
## Key Features - **Comments** — Unified across all content types, with @agent support for triggering actions - **Version History** — Automatic snapshots before agent edits, with one-click restore --- *This is a demo document created to showcase the AgentOffice document editor.*
